records.ety: [F. difier, LL. deificare, fr. L. deificus. See Deific, Deity, -fy.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Deified (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Deifying.]

defs: To make a god of; to exalt to the rank of a deity; to enroll among the deities; to apotheosize; as, Julius Cæsar was deified.
To praise or revere as a deity; to treat as an object of supreme regard; as, to deify money.
quotes: He did again so extol and deify the pope.
By our own spirits are we deified.
